theme_description: The study of subatomic particles originating from space and their interactions with cosmic phenomena.

questions.anchor_statement: Estimate the average number of solar neutrinos that pass through a human thumbnail every single second.
questions.unit: neutrinos per square centimeter per second
questions.context_clue: Despite this massive flux, neutrinos interact so weakly with matter that nearly all of them pass through your body without ever striking a single atom.

questions.anchor_statement: What is the estimated energy, in joules, of the 'Oh-My-God' particle, the most energetic cosmic ray ever detected?
questions.context_clue: This single subatomic particle carried as much kinetic energy as a baseball traveling at roughly 60 miles per hour.

questions.anchor_statement: How many Earth-masses worth of dark matter are estimated to be present within the observable volume of our own galaxy, the Milky Way?
questions.context_clue: Dark matter is estimated to make up about 85 percent of the total mass of the galaxy, yet it remains completely invisible to all electromagnetic spectrum observation.
